documents.label: The minor's birth certificate
A copy of your birth certificate
A court order
Foster care certification
A copy of your driver's license or other government-issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
A copy of the minor's Social Security card
A copy of your Social Security card

authority.notes: The form lists these under 'To show you are the minor's parent or authorized representative' as OR options — any one is sufficient.

parentId.notes: The form says 'provide one from each category' and lists these under 'For proof of your identity' — any one is sufficient.

address.notes: The form does not list a separate address-proof document category. The parent's current address is collected on the form itself.

childId.notes: The form explicitly says 'For the minor, please provide BOTH of the following' — both the minor's Social Security card and birth certificate are required.

documents.label: Child's birth certificate
Proof of guardianship, if not named on child's birth certificate
Driver's license or another government issued identification card (e.g. state ID card)
Child's Social Security card
Proof of address (bank statement, utility bill, or insurance statement)

authority.notes: The child's birth certificate satisfies authority if the parent is named on it. If the parent is not named on the birth certificate, proof of guardianship (court order) is required instead. These are mutually exclusive alternatives, not both required simultaneously.

childId.notes: The page explicitly lists both 'a copy of your child's birth certificate' and 'a copy of your child's Social Security card' as required items in the 'all of the following' list, making both required for child identity verification.

mailingAddress.notes: The page lists two addresses: standard mail (PO Box 9554) and overnight mail (701 Experian Parkway). The standard mail address is the primary mailing address. Note: this page covers checking if a minor has a credit report, not explicitly a security freeze — the same address appears to be used for all minor-related requests to Experian.

documents.label: A document issued by a federal, state, or local government agency showing proof of parentage, including a birth certificate
Certified or official copy of a birth certificate issued by the entity authorized to issue the birth certificate (parent)
An order issued by a court of law
Written communication from a county welfare/probation department certifying foster care placement
Copy of a driver's license, state-issued identification card, or any other government issued identification
A lawfully executed and valid power of attorney
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card
Social Security number or a copy of a Social Security card (parent)

authority.notes: The page lists these as alternatives under 'sufficient proof of authority' — any one is sufficient. The birth certificate (birth_child) appears here as proof of parentage and also under childId as proof of the child's identity.

parentId.notes: The page groups parent and child identity documents together under step 3, listing SSN/card, birth certificate, and government-issued ID as alternatives. Any one is sufficient for the parent.

address.notes: The protected consumer freeze section does not list any address proof requirement. Address proof is mentioned only for standard adult freeze requests (and even then is optional/not required).

childId.notes: Step 3 requires 'sufficient proof of identification' for both the parent AND the minor. The same three document types (SSN/card, birth certificate, government-issued ID) are listed for both. The id_parent ID is reused here as the page generically refers to 'government issued identification' for either party. birth_child (birth certificate) serves dual purpose: authority (proof of parentage) and childId (proof of child's identity).

mailingAddress.notes: This address is explicitly labeled on the page as 'the dedicated mailing address for protected consumer freezes at TransUnion.' It is distinct from P.O. Box 160 (Woodlyn, PA 19094), which is used for standard adult freeze add/remove requests.
